name: Khadas VIM4
description: Amlogic A311D2 with NPU, premium build

release-date.comment: Public retail availability May 2022 ($199.90 launch); pre-announced October 2021. The 3.2 TOPS NPU variant (A311D2-N0D) followed in June 2023.

status.comment: Currently sold direct by Khadas; the NPU-enabled variant is the active SKU as of 2026-04.

open-source-hardware.comment: Khadas publishes schematics for the VIM4 but not full PCB design files under an OSHW-compatible license; per RESEARCH.md guideline this does not qualify.

country-of-origin.comment: Khadas / Wesion is headquartered in Shenzhen, China.

max-clock-speed-ghz.comment: Cortex-A73 cluster runs at 2.2 GHz; A53 cluster at 2.0 GHz.

process-node.comment: Amlogic A311D2 fabricated on TSMC 12 nm per Khadas comparison document.

opengl-es-version.comment: Mali-G52 hardware supports OpenGL ES 3.2; mainline Panfrost is conformant up to ES 3.1, vendor blob covers 3.2.

vulkan-support.comment: No production Vulkan driver. PanVK is non-conformant on Mali-G52 and the Khadas vendor stack does not ship a Vulkan driver; community experiments only.

npu.comment: Current production VIM4 ships the A311D2-N0D variant with NPU enabled; original 2022 boards had the NPU disabled.

hw-video-decode.comment: A311D2 supports 4K H.264 / H.265 / VP9 decode. AV1 decode is not advertised by Khadas/Amlogic for this SoC.

hw-video-encode.comment: Hardware H.264 and H.265 encode supported by the A311D2.

ram-min-gb.comment: Only an 8 GB SKU is sold.

ram-type.comment: 8 GB LPDDR4X 64-bit @ 2016 MHz (Khadas spec page); CNX cited 2112 MHz pre-launch — Khadas product page is authoritative.

emmc.comment: 32 GB eMMC 5.1 onboard on the current SKU.

m2-nvme-slot.comment: M.2 2280 slot, single-lane PCIe 2.0, supports NVMe SSDs.

sata.comment: No native SATA port; storage attaches via M.2 PCIe or USB.

bluetooth.comment: Bluetooth 5.1 via the AP6275S combo module. Some retailer listings still cite 5.2 from pre-release marketing; the shipping module is BT 5.1.

usb-c-ports-data.comment: USB-C combines 9–20 V power input with USB 2.0 OTG data.

poe-support.comment: No PoE on the board itself; no Khadas PoE HAT exists for the VIM4 form factor.

hdmi-outputs.comment: 1× HDMI 2.1 Type-A output. The board also has a separate micro-HDMI input port for capture/conferencing.

displayport.comment: No DP output; eDP is exposed via FPC connector for embedded panels but not as a user-accessible DisplayPort.

audio-jack.comment: No 3.5 mm analog jack. Audio out is via HDMI or Bluetooth; on-board audio support is limited to digital MIC and I2S over the GPIO/M.2 headers.

rpi-compatible-header.comment: Physical 40-pin header but pinout differs from Raspberry Pi GPIO; not pin-compatible.

rpi-hat-compatible.comment: Mechanically a 40-pin header, but pinout, mounting holes (M2x4 Khadas pattern), and EEPROM auto-config do not match the Raspberry Pi HAT spec.

i2c-spi-uart.comment: I2C, UART, I2S, ADC and PWM exposed on the 40-pin header (SPI is muxed but not officially listed for this header).

power-input.value: USB-C 9–20 V (PD) or VIN header 9–20 V
power-input.comment: Wide-input range; Khadas ships a 30 W USB-C PD adapter in the kit.

idle-power-w.comment: Tom's Hardware measured 0.1 A @ 12 V at idle.

load-power-w.comment: Tom's Hardware measured up to 0.5 A @ 12 V under stress test.

cooling-required.comment: Khadas sells the VIM4 with an active cooling fan kit (3705 fan) and recommends it for sustained load; passive operation throttles quickly.

form-factor-class.comment: 82×58 mm (Khadas-VIM form factor); close to Pi-standard size but mounting holes are Khadas's own M2 pattern, so per RESEARCH.md guideline this is `custom`.

mounting-hole-pattern.comment: Four M2 mounting holes in Khadas's VIM-series pattern; not Pi-compatible.

mainline-linux-kernel.comment: Amlogic A311D2 has no upstream mainline support as of 2026-04; both Khadas and Armbian ship a vendor 5.4/5.15 fork. Mainline patches exist for some peripherals but the SoC does not boot on a stock upstream kernel.

official-os-image.comment: Khadas publishes maintained Ubuntu 22.04, Debian, and Android 11 images via OOWOW.

distros-with-images.comment: Khadas-published Ubuntu and Debian images; Armbian builds Ubuntu/Debian images on legacy 5.15.137; Manjaro ARM has historically published a VIM4 image (CoreELEC/LibreELEC also available but those are appliance distros, not in the tag set).

bootloader.comment: U-Boot (Amlogic vendor fork) chained from the Amlogic BL2/BL31 ROM. OOWOW is Khadas's installer that runs on top of U-Boot.

long-term-support.comment: Khadas has a track record of multi-year support for VIM-series boards (VIM1/3 still receive images years after release); Armbian still ships fresh builds for VIM4 in 2026. No vendor-stated EOL window.

android-support.comment: Khadas publishes an official Android 11 64-bit image; Android 14 work is in progress in the vendor tree.

msrp-base-usd.comment: Current SKU is the NPU-equipped bare board at $219.90 (price checked 2026-04-26). Original non-NPU board launched at $199.90 in May 2022.

msrp-top-usd.comment: NPU board with active-cooling kit; checked 2026-04-26.

available-regions.comment: Sold direct from Khadas worldwide and via Amazon US / Newegg / European resellers.

typical-lead-time.comment: In stock at Khadas store and major resellers as of 2026-04-26.

documentation-quality.comment: Comprehensive docs portal with hardware reference, GPIO pinout, build guides, and a published specs PDF. Loses a point because some sub-pages 404 and a full schematic/datasheet pack is not linked.

community-size.comment: Khadas runs an active forum and Discord; per RESEARCH.md thresholds Khadas falls into the 'medium' tier alongside Radxa and FriendlyELEC.

active-forum-discord.comment: Khadas vendor-run forum has a dedicated VIM4 sub-category with regular activity; Khadas also runs an official Discord.

accessory-ecosystem.comment: Official cooling kit, USB-C PSU, antennas, M2X mating SBC, DIY case, TS050 touchscreen, and OS-8A audio kit. Smaller than the Pi/Radxa ecosystems but solid first-party coverage; no broad third-party HAT scene.
